zh.lifeSummary: 當Edmund Browne於從1555年1月11日到1556年1月10日出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可費爾比時，他的父親Francis Brown52歲，母親Elizabeth John45歲。他於1570年與Elleyne Crumm在英國結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。他去世於1627年在英國諾福爾可費爾比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Edmund Browne于从1555年1月11日到1556年1月10日出生在英国英格兰諾福爾可費爾比时，他的父亲Francis Brown52岁，母亲Elizabeth John45岁。他于1570年与Elleyne Crumm在英国结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。他去世于1627年在英国諾福爾可費爾比。

nameMeanings.name: Browne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably also an Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Braune   or   Braun  .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

variants.name: Brown
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: generally a nickname referring to the color of the hair or complexion, Middle English br(o)un, from Old English brūn or Old French brun. This word is occasionally found in Old French, Middle English and Old Norse as a personal name or byname (Middle English personal name Brun, Broun, ancient Germanic Bruno, Old English Brūn, or possibly Old Norse Brúnn or Brúni). Brun- was also an ancient Germanic name-forming element. Some instances of Old English Brūn as a personal name may therefore be short forms of compound names such as Brūngar, Brūnwine, etc. As a Scottish and Irish name, it sometimes represents a translation of Gaelic Donn (see below). Brown (including in the senses below) is the fourth most frequent surname in the US. It is also very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below).
Irish and Scottish: adopted for Ó Duinn (see Dunn ) or for any of the many Irish and Scottish Gaelic names containing the element donn ‘brown-haired’ (also meaning ‘chieftain’), for example Donahue .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

nameMeanings.name: Edmund
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + mund ‘protector’. It was borne by several early royal and saintly figures, including a 9th-century king of East Anglia killed by invading Danes, allegedly for his adherence to Christianity. In the 16th and 17th centuries, there was a good deal of interchange between Edmund and Edward.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Edmund Browne was born from 11 January 1555 to 10 January 1556, in Filby, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, Francis Brown, was 52 and his mother, Elizabeth John, was 45. He married Elleyne Crumm in 1570, in England. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. He died in 1627, in Filby, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edmund Browne est né du 11 janvier 1555 au 10 janvier 1556, à Filby, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Francis Brown, avait 52 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth John, avait 45 ans. Il a épousé Elleyne Crumm en 1570, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1627, à Filby, Norfolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Edmund Browne von 11. Januar 1555 bis 10. Januar 1556 in Filby, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Francis Brown 52 und seine Mutter Elizabeth John 45. Er hat 1570 in England Elleyne Crumm geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Er starb 1627 in Filby, Norfolk, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edmund Browne è nato il da 11 gennaio 1555 a 10 gennaio 1556 nel Filby, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Francis Brown, aveva 52 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth John, aveva 45 anni. Lui sposato Elleyne Crumm nel 1570, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì nel 1627, nel Filby, Norfolk, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Edmund Browne(이)가 1555년 1월 11일부터 1556년 1월 10일까지 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 피비에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Francis Brown(은)는 52세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth John(은)는 45세였다. 그는 1570년 영국에서 Elleyne Crumm(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1627년 영국 노르폴크 피비에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edmund Browne родился с 11 января 1555 по 10 января 1556 в местности филби, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Francis Brown было 52 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth John, 45. Он женился на женщине по имени Elleyne Crumm в 1570 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Он умер в 1627 г. в местности филби, норфолк, Англия.
